programmes.show_description: The staff discover that the station is a hundred years old and decide to have a street party to celebrate. S2 E10: Ton Up
After hearing a wartime story, Gary considers the possibility of changing history. S5 E9: Love the One You're With
Mr. Bean buys a TV and a new camera but has issues with both; meanwhile, he ends up in another pickle when he goes to a magic show and a disco. E4: Mr. Bean Goes to Town
Gene and the team must protect an undercover policewoman investigating a drugs ring on their patch, and Alex continues to be haunted by the ghostly constable. S3 E4
The homeless Boswells struggle to park their caravan as Sandra flirts to make Derek jealous, but her scheme works too well and with unexpected results. S9 E5: Somewhere to Live, Somewhere to Love
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Pamela returns from London with David, Malcolm panics after nearly inviting the wrong person to dinner, and Brenda doesn't take kindly to being treated like riff-raff on her birthday. S2 E6: Dining
Sharon and Tracey work out that Dorien has an important birthday coming up - and decide that the event calls for a celebration whatever she thinks. S12 E8: Forever Young
Mr. Bean loses midnight on New Year's Eve, devises an unusual method of transporting his sales shopping home, and wreaks havoc by doing a spot of decorating. E9: Do-It-Yourself, Mr. Bean
The crew of the television holiday programme 'Wish You Were Here' - along with its presenter Judith Chalmers - are at the hotel. S3 E3: Close Up

Cissy rallies the household to help at the local soup kitchen, and Alf hatches a plan to help when Teddy is at the end of his tether over Madge. S4 E1 Yes Sir, That's My Baby
Alan's getting serious about Caroline, but her parents are well-to-do and Rigsby is far from convinced that the young scruff could possibly make the relationship work. S3 E6: The Cocktail Hour
Following a Parliamentary by-election humiliation in which he gained a mere six votes, Wolfie plans to kidnap the Conservative who beat him to the local seat and hold him ransom. S1 E5: The Hostage
Adrian loses his job, Aveline appears topless in a magazine, and Jack finds stolen property in his van. Worst of all, Billy's girlfriend is pregnant, much to Ma's disgust. S1 E6
Sandra visits the doctor longing for news of a happy event, and it dawns on Mrs Hutchinson that she will become a granny at some point... which she is not thrilled about. S9 E3: Various Kinds of Old
The workers of Hatley railway station live a gentle and predictable life, but that is rudely interrupted by some alarming news and a new station master. S1 E1: All Change
Cissy rallies the household to help at the local soup kitchen, and Alf hatches a plan to help when Teddy is at the end of his tether over Madge. S4 E1 Yes Sir, That's My Baby
Jo sees a mouse in her bedroom and refuses to sleep in there; much to Chrissy's dismay, Jo swaps with Robin while the mouse is on the loose. S3 E4: Of Mice And Women
Miss Courtney is delighted when the man she was supposed to marry 25 years ago comes back into her life. S2 E3: Brief Re-Encounter
Alf tries the patience of good natured Fred Johnson and his wife from next door by using their phone to make a very long-winded long distance call to Rita. S1 E4
Sandra visits the doctor longing for news of a happy event, and it dawns on Mrs Hutchinson that she will become a granny at some point... which she is not thrilled about. S9 E3: Various Kinds of Old
Adrian loses his job, Aveline appears topless in a magazine, and Jack finds stolen property in his van. Worst of all, Billy's girlfriend is pregnant, much to Ma's disgust. S1 E6
Ray is assigned to look after a casino owner's wife, who has become a compulsive gambler. S8 E11: The Odds Couple
The workers of Hatley railway station live a gentle and predictable life, but that is rudely interrupted by some alarming news and a new station master. S1 E1: All Change
Gary meets his son, Michael, in the present day. After seeing what could become of Michael, Gary attempts to make sure he has a brighter future. S5 E10: My Heart Belongs to Daddy
Mr. Bean gets to an appointment with the dentist after oversleeping and has trouble in the park with a bothersome fly. E5: The Trouble with Mr. Bean
DCI Gene Hunt immediately smells a rat when his former Manchester colleagues DCI Litton and DI Bevan turn up on his patch. S3 E5
Concerned by Sandra's behaviour during pregnancy, Derek and her mother step in; meanwhile, the Boswells defiantly park their caravan outside the town hall. S9 E6: The Best Things in Life Are Not Free
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Malcolm has some explaining to do when he's seen out with Susan, Pamela starts thinking about finding somewhere with a bit more space, and David finds Brenda a job in a shop. S2 E7: Moving
Sharon's living back in her council flat and Tracey's still in Chigwell with younger son Travis, but what's become of Dorien and what can bring the Birds back together? S10 E1: Gimme Shelter
Mr. Bean goes to the funfair and ends up having to watch over a baby; will the mother find them in time? E10: Mind the Baby, Mr. Bean
David and Linda use the situation to their advantage when Amy and Robert can't stand the accident-prone Neville. S3 E4: The Go-Between

Lord Meldrum decides to send Teddy overseas, Alf has plans for James, and Lady Lavender insists on a military funeral for her dead parrot. S4 E2 Requiem for a Parrot
The house is plunged into confusion and mourning when Rigsby discovers basement tenant Mr Osborne stiff as a board. S3 E7: Suddenly At Home
When a well-off customer starts chatting up Shirley in the shop, she decides to move on from Wolfie and the fearless leader may have to move out of her spare room. S1 E6: The Path of True Love
Jack gets held at gunpoint while doing a job and needs to rely on all his Boswell guile and bluff to get out of trouble. S2 E1
The Boswell family face eviction from their house as the council redevelops the local area, and Carol has a new boyfriend and really likes him... but why has he gone cool on her? S9 E4: Weeds
Harry the signalman has fattened up a lovely turkey in time for Christmas, but the turkey seems to have other plans. S1 E2: Talking Turkey
Lord Meldrum decides to send Teddy overseas, Alf has plans for James, and Lady Lavender insists on a military funeral for her dead parrot. S4 E2 Requiem for a Parrot
Chrissy receives flowers and chocolates from a secret admirer; it turns out that they were meant for Jo and that the admirer had wrongly assumed her to be Chrissy. S3 E5: Somebody Out there Likes Me
Ranjeet loses all his hard-earned money he had been saving for his mother's birthday. It seems someone from the school has found it and bet half of it on a horse race. S2 E4: Many Happy Returns
Rita comes to visit which causes friction between Alf and Else. However, when Alf criticises Margaret Thatcher the women gang up on him. S1 E5

As Arthur attempts to ingratiate himself with members of the local golf club, DS Morley offers to sell him a police transport coach. S8 E12: The Coach That Came In From The Cold
